limitations: .gitignore is a BUILD_OR_DEPLOYMENT affected public surface under executable signed-D protocol semantics; this classification does not add or qualify a public correctness, deployment, or release claim.

LIMITATION (CH-T002/E07): requirements_complete=true attests frozen CH-T002 technical and evidence requirements only: deterministic complete byte/line packet coverage of every regular Git blob on the exact signed snapshot; primary-lane assignment for every subject and distinct secondary-lane assignment for every critical subject; three independent signed automated technical review records plus signed automated lead-support review under distinct frozen keys. Human review coverage is NOT performed and NOT claimed: automated_review_only=true; independent_human_review_performed=false; named_human_review_performed=false; required_external_human_review_satisfied=null. No human, named-human, external, organizational, release, or publication review or approval is asserted or implied. Overall release status remains NO_GO; named/external human review is deferred to designated successor tasks.
Later product compatibility tasks remain OPEN.
Logs prove named commands only and may omit external platform internals.

The CH-T001 wave-gate decision is based on the pinned gate's exit status, exact ordered PASS and FAIL markers, unique gate-owned summary and epilogue chronology, and complete final success epilogue; benign child-tool diagnostics, including successful test summaries containing '0 failed' and nonempty cargo stderr, are retained and digest-bound but are not treated as independent gate failures.
The hosted-CI attempt log ZIP is retained as a separate raw Git blob capped at 2097152 compressed bytes and 4194304 expanded bytes; exceeding either bound fails closed and requires a new freeze.
The measured resource evidence applies to the recorded macOS arm64 host and the exact signed implementation snapshot.
The profile makes no cross-machine latency claim.
The resource samples describe one named host and do not establish success at a hard ceiling.
This automated lead-support review grants no release, deployment, or publication authority.

This automated review covers the exact signed snapshot only and does not report a human review.

Unknown future formats require a new protocol version.

review_finding_dispositions.finding: The lane-01 packet binds each assigned subject to exact bytes, line ranges, the frozen reviewer identity, and passing reproduction commands.
review_finding_dispositions.rationale: The signed review binds the exact implementation diff and all decisive evidence.

review_finding_dispositions.finding: The lane-02 packet has an independent primary assignment set, distinct secondary assignments for critical subjects, and digest-bound completion pointers.
review_finding_dispositions.rationale: The signed review confirms independent assignments and packet-to-subject bindings.

review_finding_dispositions.finding: The lane-03 packet and manifest reconcile to the same 395-subject set, and five measured samples stay below the declared validation bounds.
review_finding_dispositions.rationale: The signed review confirms complete reconciliation and measured validation bounds.

review_finding_dispositions.finding: The four-file implementation diff matches the frozen plan, preserves NO_GO status, and grants no release or publication authority.
review_finding_dispositions.rationale: The signed lead-support review confirms scope, claims, and authority boundaries.

L01.finding: The retained claim boundary prevents a broader inference than the evidence supports.
L01.name: Claims and scope
L01.question: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.

L02.finding: The frozen inputs, state changes, outputs, and failures define the task semantics.
L02.name: First-principles semantics
L02.question: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?
L02.residual_limitation: Automated line-by-line and relevant-context review does not constitute completed named-human or external human review.

L03.finding: The digest sets and measured distributions use explicit finite inputs.
L03.name: Mathematics and statistics
L03.question: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?
L03.residual_limitation: Classification is a bounded technical record for the exact signed F tree and does not establish runtime product correctness.

L04.finding: Canonical schemas and exact identity checks reject contradictory accepted states.
L04.name: Type and state integrity
L04.question: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?
L04.residual_limitation: Hosted workflow metadata and logs are retained byte-bound records, not cryptographic attestations issued by GitHub.

L05.finding: Adjacent signed commits, timestamps, and packet identities constrain replay and ordering.
L05.name: Time, ordering, and replay
L05.question: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?

L06.finding: Each result binds to exact commits, trees, blobs, SHA-256 values, and command streams.
L06.name: Identity and provenance
L06.question: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?
L06.residual_limitation: No release, deployment, publication, tag, GitHub release, DOI, Zenodo record, or archive authority is granted.

L07.finding: Domain-separated signatures and canonical bytes fail closed on identity mismatch.
L07.name: Authentication and cryptography
L07.question: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?
L07.residual_limitation: Successful operation at hard resource ceilings is not claimed; limits are fail-closed validation bounds.

L08.finding: Automated technical evidence grants no release, deployment, or publication authority.
L08.name: Authority and safety
L08.question: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?

L09.finding: Negative vectors cover malformed, duplicate, ambiguous, and contradictory inputs.
L09.name: Hostile inputs and parsers
L09.question: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?
L09.residual_limitation: Automated line-by-line and relevant-context review does not constitute completed named-human or external human review.

L10.finding: Byte, tree, packet, command, time, and memory limits are explicit and fail closed.
L10.name: Resource and denial-of-service bounds
L10.question: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?
L10.residual_limitation: Classification is a bounded technical record for the exact signed F tree and does not establish runtime product correctness.

L11.finding: Create-once writes, ordered commands, and exact lifecycle transitions constrain races and restart behavior.
L11.name: Concurrency and lifecycle
L11.question: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?
L11.residual_limitation: Hosted workflow metadata and logs are retained byte-bound records, not cryptographic attestations issued by GitHub.

L12.finding: Independent reconstruction reproduces the exact ledger, overlay, packets, and manifest.
L12.name: Determinism and reproducibility
L12.question: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?

L13.finding: The change adds release tooling only and makes no runtime API or compatibility claim.
L13.name: API, FFI, and SemVer
L13.question: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?
L13.residual_limitation: No release, deployment, publication, tag, GitHub release, DOI, Zenodo record, or archive authority is granted.

L14.finding: The ledger, overlay, packet builder, and independent verifier share one frozen semantic contract.
L14.name: Schema, wire, and language parity
L14.question: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?
L14.residual_limitation: Successful operation at hard resource ceilings is not claimed; limits are fail-closed validation bounds.

L15.finding: The clean capture environment excludes secret input and does not activate deployment behavior.
L15.name: Configuration and deployment
L15.question: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?

L16.finding: Digest-bound command streams distinguish success from a rejected or incomplete state.
L16.name: Observability and forensics
L16.question: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?
L16.residual_limitation: Automated line-by-line and relevant-context review does not constitute completed named-human or external human review.

L17.finding: Positive, negative, technique, reconciliation, and resource evidence support the technical scope.
L17.name: Verification and evidence quality
L17.question: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?
L17.residual_limitation: Classification is a bounded technical record for the exact signed F tree and does not establish runtime product correctness.

L18.finding: This task changes no cross-project runtime relationship and grants no ecosystem authority.
L18.name: Ecosystem composition
L18.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?
L18.residual_limitation: Hosted workflow metadata and logs are retained byte-bound records, not cryptographic attestations issued by GitHub.

L19.finding: Names, roles, limitations, and handoff boundaries remain explicit in retained records.
L19.name: Human factors and governance
L19.question: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?

L20.finding: All frozen controls and counterfactuals map to retained evidence and fail-closed checks.
L20.name: Counterfactual and quirky-case review
L20.question: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?
L20.residual_limitation: No release, deployment, publication, tag, GitHub release, DOI, Zenodo record, or archive authority is granted.
